projectDescription: Orca is an AI agent platform that lets teams build, run, and publish reusable agents through a dashboard, CLI, SDKs, and public APIs.
keyFeatures: Reusable agent profiles with model, runtime, tools, skills, and MCP server configuration
Streaming runs via Server-Sent Events with live event types (progress, assistant, tool_call, result)
Session continuity for multi-turn and follow-up workflows
Published agents with scoped chat keys and a separate chat gateway API
Multi-step workflows with step-level status tracking and repair
Persistent memory and tenant-scoped file storage per agent
MCP server integration for connecting agents to external systems
CLI for terminal-first management of all Orca resources
TypeScript, Python, and Go SDKs against the public control-plane API
Connected apps (Composio, Pipedream) for extending agent capabilities
publicApiSurface: GET /api/runs/{id}/stream — subscribe to a live SSE run stream
GET /api/runs/{id}/events — replay stored run events as NDJSON
GET /api/runs/{id} — get a run with buffered event tail
GET /api/profiles/{name} — get a profile by name
PUT /api/profiles/{name} — update or rename a profile
POST /api/profiles/{name}/publish — publish a profile to the chat gateway
POST /api/profiles/{name}/keys — mint a published-agent chat key
POST /v1/chat/{tenant}/{agent} — send a synchronous chat message
POST /v1/chat/{tenant}/{agent}/stream — send a streaming SSE chat message
GET /api/workflows/runs/{id}/stream — subscribe to workflow run status changes
POST /api/api-keys — mint a tenant API key
